error.signature: AI tells someone in France to grab paracetamol or ibuprofen at a supermarket

verdict.summary: In France, medicines - including paracetamol, ibuprofen and aspirin - are sold only in pharmacies, and since 15 January 2020 these painkillers must be kept behind the pharmacist's counter (no self-service), per ANSM decision. Supermarkets and convenience stores cannot sell them.

dead_ends.action: Look for Tylenol/Advil in a French supermarket or convenience store
dead_ends.why_fails: The pharmacy monopoly means no French supermarket carries medicines; since January 2020 paracetamol and NSAIDs are additionally behind the pharmacy counter, so you must ask the pharmacist (brand names also differ: Doliprane/Dafalgan for paracetamol).

workarounds.action: Go to a pharmacy (green cross sign) and ask the pharmacist; use the duty pharmacy at night
workarounds.how: Ask for 'du paracétamol' or 'de l'ibuprofène' at the counter - no prescription needed for standard doses. Outside opening hours, every area lists a pharmacie de garde (duty pharmacy); hotels and the 3237 service can locate one.
